mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index signals expanding manufacturing output, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% traps short-term yields near zero, forcing investors to reallocate into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform the single-series PAYEMS signal; interaction term t-stat < 2.0 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, increases in retail sales demonstrate resilient consumer demand that stabilizes corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases during UNRATE > 8.5% do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 550 thousand units signal accelerating construction activity and downstream credit demand, which lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST > 550 fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate greater than 55% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production paired with a stable or falling federal funds rate reflects a positive growth-monetary policy mix where supply expansion is not choked by tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO changes and FEDFUNDS levels is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) for predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate greater than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines faster than 0.2 percentage points do not produce average positive 5-day SPX returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low GDP growth environment, an accelerating consumer price index signals stagflationary margin compression and tightening fears, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases during GDPC1 growth below 2% do not coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
